---
title: "Causal Exaggeration: Unconfounded but Inflated Causal Estimates"
image: "images/balloon.jpg"
angle: 130
site: distill::distill_website
links:
  # - label: '`r fontawesome::fa("github")` GitHub'
  #   url: "https://github.com/vincentbagilet/causal_exaggeration"
  - label: 'Vincent Bagilet'
    url: "https://vincentbagilet.github.io/"
output:
  postcards::trestles
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)

# Learn more about creating websites with Distill at:
# https://rstudio.github.io/distill/website.html

```

Hi and welcome!

This website gathers code and additional material for the paper [Causal Exaggeration: Unconfounded but Inflated Causal Estimates](causal_exaggeration_paper.pdf).

In this paper I show that the use of causal identification strategies induces a trade-off between two biases: avoiding confounding and exaggerating true effect sizes due to a loss in statistical power.
